seoTitle: How to Get Zendesk Notifications Set Up Correctly
metaDescription: Learn how to get Zendesk notifications working, from enabling desktop alerts and sound cues to managing the notification bell and browser permissions.
intro: Missing a high-priority chat because a desktop alert never fired can hurt your response times. This guide covers configuring Zendesk's notification settings, from desktop push alerts and custom sounds to the redesigned notification bell, so you never miss an important update.

steps.heading: Open your notification settings
steps.body: Open your user settings dashboard and access the Sounds and Notifications tab.

steps.heading: Enable desktop alerts
steps.body: Check the desktop notification boxes for incoming requests, new messages, and status changes.

steps.heading: Adjust sound cues
steps.body: Adjust volume levels and pick distinct audio tones from the event dropdown menus so you can tell alerts apart.

steps.heading: Grant browser permissions
steps.body: Grant site alert permissions in your browser and disable memory saver features that could put your tab to sleep.

steps.heading: Use the notification bell
steps.body: Click the top bell icon to filter and manage unread ticket mentions and updates.

steps.heading: Verify routing prompts
steps.body: Review your active omnichannel routing rules to confirm the live Accept notification fires correctly for new assignments.

faq.question: Why am I not getting desktop alerts at all?
faq.answer: Check that your browser has granted notification permissions to Zendesk and that your tab isn't being put to sleep by a memory saver feature.

faq.question: Can I set different sounds for different event types?
faq.answer: Yes, the event dropdown menus let you assign distinct audio tones for different notification types.

faq.question: Do I have to receive offline notifications by email too?
faq.answer: No, you can opt out of repetitive offline system email summaries to keep your personal inbox clean.

faq.question: What does the bell icon actually do?
faq.answer: It opens a persistent tray of unread event cards, letting you filter and clear notification history in one place.
